name: PAY_UPGRADE_OBJECTS
description: This Table is used to capture the Object change which will be used for the upgrade process.

columns.Name: UPGRADE_OBJECT_ID
columns.Comments: UPGRADE_OBJECT_ID

columns.Name: IS_LDG_REQUIRED
columns.Comments: IS_LDG_REQUIRED

columns.Name: IS_DISABLED_IN_PRODUCTION
columns.Comments: Is disabled in production

columns.Name: IS_RERUNNABLE
columns.Comments: IS_RERUNNABLE

columns.Name: UPGRADE_OBJECT_BASE_NAME
columns.Comments: Example - EARNINGS_REP_UNIT

columns.Name: UPGRADE_MODE
columns.Comments: Type of upgrade Mandatory, Recommended, Optional

columns.Name: INCOMPATIBILITY_LEVEL
columns.Comments: INCOMPATIBILITY_LEVEL

columns.Name: UPGRADE_OBJECT_DATE
columns.Comments: Date when last upgrade is defined

columns.Name: OBJECT_ID
columns.Comments: If OBJECT_TYPE is TEMPLATE then, it will be Foreign Key to PAY_TMPLT_VERSION_F or it will be Primary Key of the object which got changed.

columns.Name: OBJECT_TYPE
columns.Comments: Object Type that we are going to Upgrade E.g : TEMPLATE

columns.Name: OBJECT_NAME
columns.Comments: Store the alternative keys of "Object Types"

columns.Name: UPGRADE_STYLE
columns.Comments: The process in which the feature can be upgraded. Automatic, Manual or Both

columns.Name: RELEASE_LEVEL
columns.Comments: Release and Patch Bundle info of when the template is updated

columns.Name: FINAL_RELEASE_LEVEL
columns.Comments: Final release and Patch Bundle info of when the template is updated

columns.Name: QUALIFYING_METHOD
columns.Comments: Similar terms to threading level in EBS

columns.Name: QUALIFYING_PROCEDURE
columns.Comments: This is the name of a PL/SQL package procedure that will identify the eligible objects for upgrade.

columns.Name: UPGRADE_METHOD
columns.Comments: This is type of the Upgrade method like java or SQL.

columns.Name: UPGRADE_PROCEDURE
columns.Comments: This is the name of a PL/SQL package procedure that will perform the upgrade. The procedure will take as input the object id that it is processing for.

columns.Name: PRE_PROCESS_METHOD
columns.Comments: This is type of the Pre Process method like java or SQL.

columns.Name: PRE_PROCESS_PROCEDURE
columns.Comments: This is the name of a PL/SQL package procedure that will be performed before the upgrade.

columns.Name: LEGISLATIVELY_ENABLED
columns.Comments: This can be set to Y or N, it can only be set to Y for patch definitions that are Global. This indicates that the localizations teams need to enable a upgrade that has been defined by the Core team.

columns.Name: LEGISLATIVE_DATA_GROUP_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign key to PER_LEGISLATIVE_DATA_GROUPS.

columns.Name: LEGISLATION_CODE
columns.Comments: Foreign key to FND_TERRITORIES.

columns.Name: MODULE_ID
columns.Comments: Seed Data Framework: indicates the module that owns the row. A module is an entry in Application Taxonomy such as a Logical Business Area. When the MODULE_ID column exists and the owner of the row is not specified, then the Seed Data Framework will not extract the row as seed data.

columns.Name: ENTERPRISE_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign key to PER_ENTERPRISES.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: SGUID
columns.Comments: The seed global unique identifier. Oracle internal use only.

columns.Name: SEED_DATA_SOURCE
columns.Comments: Source of seed data record. A value of 'BULK_SEED_DATA_SCRIPT' indicates that record was bulk loaded. Otherwise, specifies the name of the seed data file.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET1
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET1. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET2
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET2. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: PRE_HOOK_CLASS
columns.Comments: PRE_HOOK_CLASS
